name: How do I compare bids and choose the right general contractor for my project?
headline: How do I compare bids and choose the right general contractor for my project?

mainEntity.name: How do I compare bids and choose the right general contractor for my project?
mainEntity.text: How do I compare bids and choose the right general contractor for my project?

acceptedAnswer.text: You should interview and get written bids from at least three licensed general contractors before choosing one. When you compare them, first make sure they’re all bidding the same scope: same number of lights, level of finishes, appliances, windows, and so on. If one bid is much lower, it might be because they left out important items. Look at more than price. Compare: - How clearly the estimate is written - Whether allowances (for tile, flooring, etc.) seem realistic for what you want - The payment schedule - Approximate timeline - Who your main contact will be Ask each contractor about their experience with projects like yours in your specific city, such as a hillside addition in Pacifica or an interior remodel in Daly City. Ask how they handle change orders, delays, and communication. A big pitfall is choosing the contractor who “talks the best” without checking documents. Always verify license and insurance, read the entire contract, and make sure you understand what happens if you want to change something mid-project. Also consider how you feel speaking with them. You’ll be dealing with this person for weeks or months, so good communication, responsiveness, and respect for your home and neighbors are just as important as the dollar figure.
